claim: Gemma 4 comes in two tiers: workstation models (31B dense and 26B MoE with 4B active) designed for local development, and edge models (E2B and E4B) designed to run on phones and edge devices
actionable: Evaluate Gemma 4 based on your deployment target: use workstation models for local development environments or edge models if targeting mobile/IoT devices

claim: Gemma 4 uses 128 expert MoE architecture with only 8 experts activated per token plus one shared expert, delivering roughly 27B model intelligence at 4B compute cost
actionable: When evaluating MoE models, verify the total parameters vs. active parameters and expert count to understand actual computational requirements vs. model capacity

claim: Gemma 4 natively supports audio transcription and speech-to-translated-text (translate audio from one language to another in single model), not just bolted-on external ASR
actionable: Use Gemma 4 edge models for multilingual voice applications without needing separate Whisper or ASR pipelines when transcription and translation are needed together

claim: Gemma 4 edge model vision encoder reduced from 300-350M parameters to 150M parameters compared to Gemma 3N, improving speed and reducing device storage needs
actionable: When deploying vision models locally, prioritize models with optimized encoders; smaller vision encoder sizes enable faster inference and lower memory footprint

claim: Gemma 4 function calling is integrated at the architecture level (from scratch) for multi-turn agentic flows with multiple tools, not just hoping model follows prompt templates
actionable: Use Gemma 4 for agentic workflows since function calling is natively supported; this is more reliable than models requiring careful prompt engineering for tool use

claim: Gemma 4 is released under Apache 2.0 license without restrictions, allowing commercial deployment and fine-tuning unlike previous Gemma 3 which had custom license with competitive clauses
actionable: When selecting open-source models, verify Apache 2.0 license is used; this enables unrestricted commercial use, modification, and deployment without legal complications

claim: Gemma 4 can serve larger models (31B dense) in serverless on Google Cloud Run using G4 GPUs (RTX 6000 Pro with 96GB VRAM) that scale to zero when not in use
actionable: Use Cloud Run with G4 GPUs for cost-effective serving of large Gemma models; serverless scaling eliminates idle compute costs

claim: Gemma 4 edge models support thinking (chain-of-thought reasoning) with enable_thinking=true parameter, allowing complex reasoning on edge devices with 128K context window
actionable: Enable thinking mode in Gemma 4 edge models for tasks requiring multi-step reasoning; pass enable_thinking=true to activate this capability
